advisory.title: /pair approve command path omitted caller scope subsetting and reopened device pairing escalation
advisory.description:
## Summary

The `/pair approve` command path called device approval without forwarding caller scopes into the core approval check.

## Impact

A caller that held pairing privileges but not admin privileges could approve a pending device request asking for broader scopes, including admin access.

## Affected Component

`extensions/device-pair/index.ts, src/infra/device-pairing.ts`

## Fixed Versions

- Affected: `<= 2026.3.24`
- Patched: `>= 2026.3.28`

## Fix

Fixed by commit `4ee4960de2330b5322127f925f3687dc6f105be1` (`Pairing: forward caller scopes during approval`).

introducingCommits.subject: tests(feishu): reload chat tool after mock reset

timeline.notes: This case uses the immediate parent of fix commit 4ee4960de2330b5322127f925f3687dc6f105be1 as the last known vulnerable snapshot. Exact introduction commit mapping is still pending.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should detect brokenauthz when scanning vulnerableHead. This case uses the last known vulnerable pre-fix commit pending exact introduction mapping.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_is_fix_parent

checks.name: baseline_precedes_vulnerable_head
checks.details: Using direct parent 6eb82fba3cbfd0e50b179c1fada92e1e22dce7fa of vulnerableHead 2e23d44491c621e293925c8a624f6c50c1b762fe as a temporary baseline until exact introduction mapping is completed.
